title: Reference Architecture Definition and Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for the Gateway Platform with DID/VC and Blockchain
description: Produce an end-to-end, implementation-ready architecture document and a numbered set of Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) covering all critical components (Discovery Service, Sentinels, Blockchain Trust Layer), data flows, trust boundaries, and irreversible decisions (DID/VC/VP model, revocation, onboarding, multi-instance, migration). This document becomes the binding implementation contract for all engineering teams.
objectives: Produce an implementable architecture (C4 + sequence diagrams) for all critical flows
Lock key decisions and rejected alternatives (ADRs) so implementation is deterministic
Clearly establish the separation: Discovery as orchestration/config, Sentinels as enforcement, Blockchain as trust anchor

implementation_guidance.overview: Write a formal Architecture Reference Document and a set of numbered ADRs that cover all architectural decisions, component responsibilities, data flows, and security invariants. This document must be sufficiently detailed that engineering teams can implement without additional design decisions.
steps: Step 1 - Create the Architecture Reference Document: Write a document structured into sections: (1) System Overview, (2) Component Catalogue (Discovery, Sentinel-Producer, Sentinel-Consumer, Blockchain Trust Layer, Secret Storage), (3) Responsibilities Matrix mapping each component to its functions, (4) API Contract Summary (references to detailed specs), (5) Data Flow Catalogue, (6) Network Topology, (7) Trust Boundaries, (8) Security Invariants.

Step 2 - Produce C4 diagrams: Level 1 (System Context): show Government Entity A, Government Entity B, Central Discovery, Blockchain, and external actors. Level 2 (Container): expand each entity to show Consumer Service, Consumer Sentinel, Discovery Service, Producer Sentinel, Producer Service, and the Trust Layer contracts. Level 3 (Component): for each service, show internal modules (e.g., Producer Sentinel: VP Verifier, Policy Engine, Revocation Manager, Proxy).

Step 3 - Produce sequence diagrams using PlantUML or Mermaid for: (a) Application/service registration, (b) Sentinel onboarding, (c) VC issuance, (d) Consumer-to-Producer request execution, (e) Credential revocation, (f) Key/credential rotation, (g) VM migration of a Sentinel, (h) Multi-instance Sentinel join.

Step 4 - Define message fields for each sequence diagram: for every arrow in the sequence diagram, specify the exact JSON payload (field names, types, required/optional). This ensures engineers do not make ad-hoc decisions.

Step 5 - Write numbered ADRs. Each ADR must contain: ID, Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences (positive/negative), Rejected Alternatives. Required ADRs: ADR-001 DID Method selection, ADR-002 VC encoding format (JWT-VC vs JSON-LD), ADR-003 Revocation mechanism (Bitstring Status List), ADR-004 Request anti-replay strategy (DPoP-inspired), ADR-005 Blockchain network selection, ADR-006 Secret storage architecture, ADR-007 Environment isolation model (separate DIDs per env), ADR-008 Transport baseline (HTTP with DIDComm as optional adapter).

Step 6 - Define Security Invariants as a numbered list. Each invariant must be testable. Examples: SI-001: A request MUST be rejected if the credential status is stale beyond Δ; SI-002: An enrollment token is one-time use and cannot be replayed; SI-003: A VP proof MUST be bound to the exact audience DID and environment; SI-004: Discovery makes no final authorization decisions; SI-005: A disabled issuer's VCs MUST be rejected immediately.
Step 7 - Classify all data in the document as: PUBLIC (can be in logs), INTERNAL (team-confidential), SECRET (must be redacted). Ensure no secret material appears in document body.

project_structure.description: Files to create as outputs of this task
files: docs/architecture/README.md — index of all architecture documents

domain_entities.description: Key entities that the architecture document must define precisely

entities.name: GovernmentEntity
entities.description: An independent government organization operating its own Sentinel(s)
fields: entity_id: string — unique identifier (e.g., RO-MFP, RO-MAI)
sentinel_dids: array<string> — DIDs of registered Sentinels

entities.name: Sentinel
fields: did: string — the Sentinel's W3C DID (e.g., did:key:z6Mk...)
service_id: string — the service it represents
public_key: JWK — the public key material for the DID

entities.name: TrustBoundary
entities.description: A demarcation of what is trusted and what is not
fields: trusts: array<string> — which components it trusts
enforcement_point: string — which component enforces this boundary

discovery_to_sentinel.description: Discovery provides configuration and credentials to Sentinels

consumer_sentinel_to_producer_sentinel.description: Consumer attaches a signed proof (ProofClaims JWS) and VP to each request
consumer_sentinel_to_producer_sentinel.mechanism: HTTP headers: Authorization: SentinelProof <jws> and SentinelVP: <jwt-vp>

sentinel_to_blockchain.description: Read-only queries for trust state; Discovery writes anchors
sentinel_to_blockchain.contracts_referenced: IssuerRegistry, TrustPolicyRegistry, StatusRegistry, ServiceRegistry — defined in TASK-014 through TASK-017

security_invariant_format.description: Standard format for each security invariant

example.statement: A request MUST be rejected if the credential status is stale beyond Δ

adr_format.description: Standard ADR format to use in all ADR files

security_constraints: Discovery must never be described as an authorization authority — explicitly document that final enforcement is at Producer Sentinel
All trust decisions must reference a verifiable on-chain anchor or a signed artifact
The document must clearly state that transporting a request through Discovery does NOT imply authorization
Sensitive data classifications must be applied to every data element in sequence diagrams

technical_requirements: PlantUML or Mermaid for sequence and C4 diagrams
W3C DID Core v1.0 for terminology and concepts
W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model v2.0 for VC/VP concepts
ADR template (e.g., MADR or Michael Nygard format)
acceptance_criteria: An approved Architecture Reference Document exists with all required sections
At least 8 numbered ADRs exist, each with context, decision, and rejected alternatives
All critical flows have sequence diagrams with message fields specified
Security invariants are listed, numbered, and referenced in subsequent implementation tasks
All document data is classified as PUBLIC / INTERNAL / SECRET
Engineering teams can begin implementing without making new architectural decisions
